seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in Azure AI Agent Service Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in Azure AI Agent Service agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Azure AI Agent Service, PII Filtering for AI Agents manifests as unfiltered sensitive data leaking through agent responses, tool outputs, and grounding sources due to insufficient validation in the processing pipeline. Agents integrating Azure OpenAI models, Bing grounding, and file search often expose PII from enterprise documents or user queries without enterprise policy enforcement. This creates compliance risks in regulated industries using Azure's agent platform.

howItManifests.scenario: Agent processes customer support tickets with embedded PII
howItManifests.symptom: Azure OpenAI responses contain unredacted SSNs, emails, and phone numbers
howItManifests.impact: GDPR/HIPAA violations with audit trail exposure

howItManifests.scenario: Bing Search grounding returns enterprise contact data
howItManifests.symptom: Search snippets include employee emails and internal phone directories
howItManifests.impact: Internal directory enumeration by external attackers

howItManifests.scenario: File search retrieves HR documents with employee PII
howItManifests.symptom: Azure-specific metadata includes document author emails and timestamps
howItManifests.impact: Employee privacy exposure through metadata leakage

howItManifests.scenario: Tool execution returns database query results with customer data
howItManifests.symptom: SQL tool outputs unfiltered customer names, addresses, and account numbers
howItManifests.impact: Mass PII exposure through agent conversation history

detection.name: PII Regex Scanner for Agent Responses
detection.description: Scans Azure AI Agent Service streaming responses for common PII patterns using regex
detection.code:
import re

from azure.ai.projects import AgentSession

PII_PATTERNS = [
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b',  # SSN
    r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',  # Email
    r'\b\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}\b',  # Phone
    r'\b\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}\b'   # Date
]

def scan_agent_response(content: str) -> list:
    findings = []
    for pattern in PII_PATTERNS:
        matches = re.finditer(pattern, content)
        for match in matches:
            findings.append({
                'type': 'PII',
                'pattern': pattern,
                'match': match.group(),
                'location': match.span()
            })
    return findings

# Usage in Azure Agent pipeline
session = AgentSession(agent_id='your-agent-id')
response = session.run('Process customer ticket #123')

pii_detected = scan_agent_response(response.content)
if pii_detected:
    print(f'🚨 PII detected: {len(pii_detected)} matches')

detection.name: Azure AI Projects Content Filter Monitor
detection.description: Monitors Azure AI Projects agent threads for PII using content safety API
detection.code:
from azure.ai.content_safety import ContentSafetyClient
from azure.core.credentials import AzureKeyCredential

client = ContentSafetyClient(
    endpoint="https://your-cs-endpoint.cognitiveservices.azure.com/",
    credential=AzureKeyCredential("your-key")
)

def check_pii_safety(text: str):
    request = AnalyzeTextOptions(text=text)
    request.categories = ["PiiEntity"]

    response = client.analyze_text(request)
    pii_entities = []

    if response.pii_result and response.pii_result.redacted_text:
        pii_count = sum(len(entity.text) for entity in response.pii_result.entities)
        if pii_count > 0:
            print(f"🚨 {pii_count} PII entities detected")
            return True
    return False

# Integrate with agent response
agent_response = "Customer John Doe (john.doe@company.com) SSN: 123-45-6789"
if check_pii_safety(agent_response):
    raise ValueError("PII detected - blocking response")

fixes.name: Presidio PII Redactor Middleware
fixes.description: Integrates Microsoft Presidio anonymizer into Azure AI Agent pipeline
fixes.code:
from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer import AnonymizerEngine
from azure.ai.projects import AgentSession

analyzer = AnalyzerEngine()
anonymizer = AnonymizerEngine()

def redact_pii(text: str) -> str:
    results = analyzer.analyze(text=text, language='en')
    anonymized = anonymizer.anonymize(text=text, analyzer_results=results)
    return anonymized.text

class PIIAgentMiddleware:
    def __init__(self, agent_session: AgentSession):
        self.session = agent_session
    
    async def run_with_redaction(self, query: str):
        # Redact input
        clean_query = redact_pii(query)
        
        # Get agent response
        response = await self.session.run(clean_query)
        
        # Redact output
        clean_response = redact_pii(response.content)
        response.content = clean_response
        
        return response

# Usage
agent = AgentSession('your-agent-id')
middleware = PIIAgentMiddleware(agent)
response = await middleware.run_with_redaction("Customer SSN 123-45-6789 needs help")

fixes.name: Azure Content Safety Policy Enforcement
fixes.description: Enforces PII blocking at Azure Content Safety level for agent outputs
fixes.code:
from azure.ai.content_safety import ContentSafetyClient

class AzurePIIGuard:
    def __init__(self, endpoint: str, key: str):
        self.client = ContentSafetyClient(
            endpoint=endpoint,
            credential=AzureKeyCredential(key)
        )
    
    async def filter_agent_output(self, content: str) -> str:
        poller = self.client.begin_analyze_text(
            text=content,
            categories=["PiiEntity"]
        )
        
        result = poller.result()
        
        if result.pii_result and result.pii_result.redacted_text:
            print("✅ PII automatically redacted by Azure Content Safety")
            return result.pii_result.redacted_text
        
        return content

# Pipeline integration
guard = AzurePIIGuard("https://your-cs.cognitiveservices.azure.com", "your-key")

agent_response = "Contact john.doe@company.com at 555-123-4567"

safe_response = await guard.filter_agent_output(agent_response)
print(safe_response)  # "Contact <PII> at <PII>"

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as middleware in the Azure AI Agent Service processing pipeline, automatically scanning all agent inputs, tool outputs, and grounding data for PII exposure. It uses Azure-native Content Safety APIs combined with regex pattern matching to detect and block PII in real-time across agent threads. Vex provides automated remediation through Presidio anonymization and policy enforcement, with full audit trails for compliance reporting.

The Vex SDK (`pip install vex-sdk`) instruments Azure AI Projects sessions with zero-code changes, capturing telemetry from Bing grounding, file search, and tool executions. It generates compliance reports showing PII exposure rates and automatically applies fixes through middleware injection. Vex's agent reliability scoring flags high-risk agents before production deployment.

Vex continuously monitors token limits and grounding freshness alongside PII, providing comprehensive agent reliability for Azure deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
import vex
from azure.ai.projects import AgentSession

# Vex auto-instruments Azure AI Agent Service
@vex.instrument(guards=['pii-filtering'])
def safe_agent_session(agent_id: str):
    session = AgentSession(agent_id=agent_id)
    
    @vex.guard('pii-redaction')
    async def run_with_protection(self, query: str):
        # Vex automatically scans and redacts
        result = await self.session.run(query)
        return result
    
    session.run_with_protection = run_with_protection.__wrapped__
    return session

# Zero-config integration
agent = safe_agent_session('your-agent-id')

# Vex dashboard shows:
# - PII detection rate: 0.2%
# - Auto-redactions: 47
# - Compliance score: 98/100

response = await agent.run_with_protection("Customer John SSN 123-45-6789")

# View results
print(vex.report())
